events.title: Bang Si-hyuk Assembles BTS Trainees
events.detail: Big Hit Entertainment CEO Bang Si-hyuk begins recruiting members for a new hip-hop oriented boy group. RM (Kim Nam-joon) is the first recruit, signing as a trainee in 2010. Other members are gradually added through auditions, including J-Hope, Suga, Jin, Jimin, V, and Jungkook.

events.title: BTS Officially Debuts with '2 Cool 4 Skool'
events.detail: Bangtan Sonyeondan (BTS) officially debuts on Mnet's M Countdown with their first single album '2 Cool 4 Skool' and lead single 'No More Dream.' The seven-member group — RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V, and Jungkook — is signed to Big Hit Entertainment under the creative direction of Bang Si-hyuk.

events.title: 'O!RUL8,2?' Mini Album Released
events.detail: BTS releases their second extended play 'O!RUL8,2?' with the lead single 'N.O.' The album addresses the pressures Korean youth face from societal expectations around education and career paths, establishing the group's signature socially conscious lyrical approach.

events.title: 'Skool Luv Affair' Charts on Billboard World Albums
events.detail: BTS releases their third EP 'Skool Luv Affair' featuring the single 'Boy In Luv.' It becomes their first album to chart on the Billboard World Albums chart, marking their first foothold in the US market and signaling growing international interest.

events.title: Debut Studio Album 'Dark & Wild' Released
events.detail: BTS releases their debut full-length studio album 'Dark & Wild' featuring 14 tracks. The album enters the US Billboard Heatseekers chart and demonstrates the group's expansion beyond their school-era concept into darker, more mature themes.

events.title: Japan Debut Album 'Wake Up' Expands Asian Reach
events.detail: BTS releases their first Japanese studio album 'Wake Up,' expanding their presence in the Japanese market — a crucial revenue source for K-pop acts. The album includes Japanese versions of Korean hits alongside new material.

events.title: 'The Most Beautiful Moment in Life Pt. 1' Released
events.detail: BTS releases the first part of the 'Hwa Yang Yeon Hwa' (The Most Beautiful Moment in Life) series. The mini album features 'I Need U,' exploring themes of youth, pain, and fleeting beauty. The concept marks BTS's artistic maturation beyond the school trilogy.

events.title: 'HYYH Pt. 2' Achieves First Billboard 200 Entry
events.detail: BTS releases 'The Most Beautiful Moment in Life Pt. 2,' featuring the single 'Run.' The album marks BTS's first ever entry on the Billboard 200 US chart at No. 171, signaling a growing Western fanbase and hinting at their future global dominance.

events.title: 'Wings' Debuts at No. 26 on Billboard 200 — K-pop Record
events.detail: BTS releases their second studio album 'Wings,' featuring solo and sub-unit tracks for the first time. The album enters the Billboard 200 at No. 26 — the highest-charting K-pop album in US history at the time — demonstrating their explosive global growth.

events.title: BTS Wins First Billboard Music Award, Beating Justin Bieber
events.detail: BTS wins the Top Social Artist award at the Billboard Music Awards in Las Vegas, defeating Justin Bieber who had won the title for six consecutive years. This is the first time a K-pop act wins at the BBMAs and marks BTS's breakthrough into US mainstream consciousness.

events.title: 'Love Yourself: Her' Peaks at No. 7 on Billboard 200
events.detail: BTS releases 'Love Yourself: Her' featuring global hit 'DNA,' which charts in 54 countries simultaneously. The album debuts at No. 7 on the Billboard 200 — the highest position ever for a K-pop album at that time — and sets streaming records across all major platforms.

events.title: BTS Becomes First Korean Act to Perform at the AMAs
events.detail: BTS becomes the first Korean act to perform at the American Music Awards, performing 'DNA' to an audience of 10,000+ in Los Angeles. The nationally televised performance introduces BTS to tens of millions of American viewers for the first time.

events.title: 'Love Yourself: Tear' — First K-pop Album to Top Billboard 200
events.detail: BTS's fourth studio album 'Love Yourself: Tear' debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard 200, making BTS the first K-pop act to ever top the US albums chart. The album sells over 135,000 album-equivalent units in its first week in the US alone.

events.title: 'Love Yourself: Answer' Reaches No. 1 — Second Consecutive Billboard Topper
events.detail: BTS releases 'Love Yourself: Answer,' a repackage album capping the Love Yourself series. It becomes their second consecutive No. 1 album on the Billboard 200, including the new track 'Idol' which celebrates Korean cultural identity.

events.title: Love Yourself World Tour Launches in Seoul
events.detail: BTS begins the 'Love Yourself World Tour' with sold-out shows at the Olympic Main Stadium in Seoul. The tour ultimately spans 62 concerts in 14 countries across North America, Europe, and Asia, attended by over 2 million fans and grossing $196.4 million.

events.title: RM Addresses 73rd UN General Assembly: 'Speak Yourself'
events.detail: BTS leader RM addresses the 73rd UN General Assembly in New York City on behalf of UNICEF's Generation Unlimited initiative. His speech — urging young people to 'speak yourself' — is viewed by tens of millions online and marks BTS's entry into global diplomatic and humanitarian engagement.

events.title: 'Map of the Soul: Persona' — Third No. 1 Billboard 200 Album
events.detail: BTS releases 'Map of the Soul: Persona' featuring 'Boy With Luv' ft. Halsey. The album debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 and sells 3.4 million copies in South Korea in two months — the best-selling album in South Korean history at the time.

events.title: 'Speak Yourself' Tour Breaks Rose Bowl All-Time Gross Record
events.detail: BTS begins the 'Love Yourself: Speak Yourself' stadium tour with shows at the Rose Bowl in Pasadena, CA. The two-night run becomes the highest-grossing event in Rose Bowl history with 100,000+ attendees across two nights.

events.title: BTS Sets Record as First Korean Act to Sell Out US Arenas
events.detail: During their early 2019 US dates, BTS sells out every US arena on the Love Yourself World Tour, consistently drawing 18,000–20,000 per night. They become the first Korean act to headline and sell out major US arenas at this scale across multiple cities.

events.title: BTS Becomes First Korean Act to Sell Out Wembley Stadium
events.detail: BTS sells out Wembley Stadium in London for two consecutive nights (June 1–2, 2019). They become the first Korean act and first non-English-speaking act to headline Wembley Stadium — a legendary milestone in global music history.

events.title: Love Yourself World Tour Concludes: $196.4M Gross
events.detail: The Love Yourself World Tour concludes after 62 concerts in 14 countries, attended by over 2 million fans worldwide. The tour grosses $196.4 million, placing BTS among the world's top-grossing touring acts and setting a new standard for K-pop live performances.

era: Map of the Soul & COVID-19 Pivot (2020)

events.title: 'Map of the Soul: 7' Sells 4.1 Million Copies in 9 Days
events.detail: BTS releases 'Map of the Soul: 7' — their fourth consecutive No. 1 album on the Billboard 200. The album sells 4.1 million copies worldwide in its first nine days, breaking all K-pop records. The 39-date Map of the Soul Tour is subsequently cancelled due to COVID-19.

events.title: Map of the Soul World Tour Cancelled Due to COVID-19
events.detail: BTS announces the cancellation of their entire 39-show Map of the Soul World Tour due to the global COVID-19 pandemic. The cancellation affects hundreds of thousands of ticket holders and represents a massive financial loss, forcing the group to pivot to virtual concerts.

events.title: 'Dynamite' Debuts at No. 1 — First Korean Act Atop Billboard Hot 100
events.detail: 'Dynamite,' BTS's first fully English-language single, debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — making BTS the first Korean act ever to top the US singles chart. Released August 21, it spends three weeks at No. 1 and becomes a global pandemic anthem, viewed 100M+ times on YouTube in its first 24 hours.

events.title: 'Life Goes On' — First Korean-Language Song to Debut at No. 1 on Hot 100
events.detail: 'Life Goes On' from their album BE debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — the first Korean-language song ever to debut at number one on the chart. The pandemic-themed song resonates globally as a message of hope and resilience.

events.title: 'BE' Album Released — Members Direct Their Own Chapters
events.detail: BTS releases 'BE,' an album with significant member creative control — each member choosing their own concepts, aesthetics, and a track to direct. Created during COVID-19, the album reflects on longing, connection, and what it means to be an artist during a global crisis.

events.title: BTS Named IFPI Global Recording Artist of the Year
events.detail: BTS is named the IFPI Global Recording Artist of the Year for 2020 — the most consumed recording act worldwide across all formats. This is the first time any Korean artist or group wins this distinction, representing a defining moment for K-pop globally.

events.title: BTS Address 75th UN General Assembly on COVID-19
events.detail: BTS deliver a pre-recorded video message at the 75th UN General Assembly, speaking to global youth on hope and resilience during the COVID-19 pandemic — their second UN appearance in three years.

events.title: BTS Receives First Grammy Nomination for 'Dynamite'
events.detail: BTS receives their first Grammy Award nomination — Best Pop Duo/Group Performance for 'Dynamite' at the 63rd Grammy Awards. While they do not win, it marks the first Grammy recognition for any Korean act in history.

events.title: First Korean Act to Perform at the Grammy Awards
events.detail: BTS performs 'Dynamite' at the 63rd Grammy Awards ceremony at Gyeongbokgung Palace in Seoul, becoming the first Korean act to perform on the Grammy stage. The global broadcast is viewed by hundreds of millions of viewers worldwide.

events.title: 'Butter' Spends 10 Weeks at No. 1 on Hot 100
events.detail: BTS releases 'Butter,' which spends a combined 10 weeks at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — a 2021 record. They also become the fastest group to accumulate four US No. 1 singles since the Jackson 5 in 1970.

events.title: 'Permission to Dance' — Fifth Hot 100 No. 1, Matching Beatles Milestone
events.detail: 'Permission to Dance' replaces 'Butter' at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100, giving BTS their fifth number-one hit. They become the first act since The Beatles to have their first five No. 1 singles all debut at the top of the chart.

events.title: BTS Address 76th UN General Assembly as Presidential Envoys
events.detail: BTS speaks at the UN's SDG Moment at the 76th General Assembly in New York. The group is appointed South Korea's Special Presidential Envoys for Future Generations and Culture by President Moon Jae-in — their most significant diplomatic role to date.

events.title: 'My Universe' with Coldplay — Sixth Hot 100 No. 1
events.detail: 'My Universe,' a collaboration between BTS and Coldplay, debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — the first collaboration between two groups to debut at No. 1. BTS becomes the fastest act since The Beatles to accumulate six US No. 1 singles.

events.title: SoFi Stadium Run Grosses $33.3M — Biggest Boxscore in Nearly a Decade
events.detail: BTS plays four sold-out nights at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, CA (Nov 27–Dec 2, 2021) as part of 'Permission to Dance on Stage.' The run grosses $33.3 million from 214,000 tickets — the biggest concert boxscore by any act in nearly a decade.

era: PTD On Stage, AMA Win & Hiatus (2021–2022)

events.title: First Asian Act to Win AMA Artist of the Year
events.detail: BTS wins Artist of the Year at the American Music Awards 2021 — the first Asian act ever to win this prestigious honor. They also win Favorite Pop Duo or Group and Favorite Pop Song, completing a three-award sweep at the ceremony.

events.title: BTS Wins IFPI Global Recording Artist of the Year for Second Consecutive Year
events.detail: BTS is named IFPI Global Recording Artist of the Year for 2021, becoming the first act to win in two consecutive years since the award's inception. The achievement underscores their unparalleled global cultural reach.

events.title: 'Permission to Dance on Stage' Las Vegas Concludes to 200,000+ Fans
events.detail: BTS closes their 'Permission to Dance on Stage' concert series with four sold-out nights at Allegiant Stadium in Las Vegas (April 8–16, 2022), totaling over 200,000 attendees. These are their first in-person US concerts since 2019.

events.title: 'Proof' Released; BTS Announces Hiatus for Solo Chapter
events.detail: BTS releases 'Proof,' a three-disc anthology album marking the close of 'Chapter 1.' At their FESTA dinner video, members announce a hiatus to pursue individual solo activities. Suga says directly: 'We're going on a hiatus now.' HYBE's stock drops approximately 25% the following trading day.

events.title: Big Hit Entertainment Rebrands as HYBE; IPO Raises $819M
events.detail: Big Hit Entertainment rebrands as HYBE Corporation following its October 2020 IPO on the Korea Stock Exchange, which raised approximately $819 million and valued the company at $4 billion. BTS holds a significant ownership stake, making them among the wealthiest pop stars in South Korea.

events.title: J-Hope Releases First BTS Solo LP; Headlines Lollapalooza
events.detail: J-Hope releases 'Jack in the Box,' the first full-length solo studio album by any BTS member. He headlines Lollapalooza in Chicago on July 31, 2022 — the first South Korean artist ever to headline the iconic festival, playing to 100,000 attendees.

events.title: RM Releases Debut Solo Album 'Indigo'
events.detail: RM releases 'Indigo,' his first solo studio album featuring collaborations with Erykah Badu, Anderson .Paak, and Epik High's Tablo. Described as a document of his late twenties, the album debuts in the top five of multiple global charts.

events.title: Jimin's 'Like Crazy' — First Korean Solo Artist Atop Billboard Hot 100
events.detail: Jimin releases his debut solo album 'FACE,' whose lead single 'Like Crazy' debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 — making Jimin the first Korean solo artist ever to top the chart. He also becomes the first Korean solo artist to top the Billboard Artist 100.

events.title: Suga Releases 'D-DAY' as Agust D; Launches World Tour
events.detail: Suga (as Agust D) releases 'D-Day,' completing his Agust D trilogy. The album features IU, J-Hope, and the late Ryuichi Sakamoto. The accompanying 28-show 'D-Day' world tour ranks 9th on Pollstar's 2023 Top 20 Global Concert Tours by average gross.

events.title: V Releases Debut Solo Album 'Layover'
events.detail: V (Kim Tae-hyung) releases his debut solo mini album 'Layover,' a six-track jazz/R&B/pop project with music videos for all tracks. The album showcases V's soulful vocal range and establishes his distinct artistic identity outside the group.

events.title: Jungkook's 'GOLDEN' Becomes Best-Selling K-pop Solo Album in US History
events.detail: Jungkook releases 'GOLDEN' featuring Jack Harlow, Major Lazer, and Latto. Within three weeks, it becomes the best-selling album by a K-pop solo artist in US history by total units, surpassing his bandmates' solo releases.

events.title: RM Releases Second Solo Album 'Right Place, Wrong Person'
events.detail: RM releases his second solo studio album 'Right Place, Wrong Person' while still serving in the military. The album features Little Simz, Domi & JD Beck, and Moses Sumney, reflecting on identity and belonging.

events.title: Jimin's 'MUSE' Surpasses 1 Billion Spotify Streams
events.detail: Jimin releases his second solo album 'MUSE,' whose lead single 'Who' debuts at No. 1 on the Billboard Global 200. The album surpasses 1 billion Spotify streams, making Jimin the first K-pop solo artist with two albums reaching that streaming milestone.

events.title: Jin Becomes First BTS Member to Enlist in Military
events.detail: Jin (Kim Seok-jin) enlists for mandatory South Korean military service on December 13, 2022, becoming the first BTS member to enter the armed forces. The group had been granted a special deferral; with the 'Proof' chapter complete, all members planned to serve.

events.title: J-Hope Enlists; Serves as Assistant Drill Instructor
events.detail: J-Hope enlists on April 18, 2023, serving as an assistant drill instructor at a basic training center. His enlistment follows a banner year for his solo career including Lollapalooza and 'Jack in the Box.'

events.title: Suga Enlists as Social Service Worker Due to Shoulder Surgery
events.detail: Suga enlists as an alternative social service worker on September 22, 2023, due to a prior shoulder surgery that prevents active duty. He is the only BTS member to serve in an alternative capacity rather than active military duty.

events.title: RM, V, Jimin & Jungkook Enlist — All 7 Members Now Serving
events.detail: RM and V enlist simultaneously on December 11; Jimin and Jungkook enlist together on December 12, 2023. With all seven members now in military service, BTS enters the longest pause in its career. RM serves in the Army Band; V in the 2nd Division Special Duty Team.

events.title: Jin Discharged — All Six BTS Members Attend His Greeting
events.detail: Jin completes 18 months of mandatory military service and is discharged on June 12, 2024. All six remaining members — on approved military leave — attend his discharge in a historic moment. Jin later releases his debut solo album 'Happy' (November 2024).

events.title: J-Hope Discharged; Launches Solo World Tour in Early 2025
events.detail: J-Hope is discharged from military service on October 17, 2024. Post-discharge, he releases singles and launches his 'Hope on the Stage' solo world tour in early 2025, playing across Asia and North America.

events.title: Suga Discharged — All 7 BTS Members Complete Military Service
events.detail: Suga completes his alternative social service on June 21, 2025, the last BTS member to finish mandatory service. Within 11 days, all seven members were discharged. HYBE hangs 'We are back' banners on their headquarters. The full reunion is at hand.

events.title: Jin Releases 'Happy' — All 7 Members Now Have Solo Top 10 Billboard Albums
events.detail: Jin releases his debut solo studio album 'Happy' featuring Gary Barlow, Wendy (Red Velvet), and Taka (One Ok Rock). Debuting at No. 4 on the Billboard 200, Jin becomes the last BTS member to release a solo LP, and all seven members have now achieved Billboard 200 top 10 placements.

events.title: BTS Celebrates 12th Debut Anniversary Ahead of Full Reunion
events.detail: BTS celebrates 12 years since their June 13, 2013 debut. With most members discharged or days away from discharge, the anniversary is marked by ARMY worldwide and serves as a prelude to the full group's imminent reunion.

events.title: All 7 Members Reunite on Weverse; New Album & World Tour Announced
events.detail: All seven BTS members appear together in a Weverse livestream watched by over 7 million simultaneous viewers. They announce a new group album and world tour, confirming they have begun collaborative work for the first time since their 2022 hiatus.

events.title: J-Hope Launches 'Hope on the Stage' World Tour Post-Discharge
events.detail: J-Hope launches his 'Hope on the Stage' solo world tour in February 2025, his first major headline tour following his October 2024 military discharge. The tour spans Asia and North America, marking the first major BTS solo live activity of the reunion era.

events.title: BTS Releases First Group Album in Over 5 Years
events.detail: BTS releases their new group studio album — their first since 'BE' in November 2020, a gap of over five years. The release marks their full return as a complete group following all seven members completing mandatory South Korean military service.
